title: Crispy Filipino Tapioca Stir-fry
description: A buttery and crispy brunch-mood dish featuring red pepper flakes, sautéed with sweet-tangy tapioca pearls for a delightful snack or appetizer.
instructions: Rinse tapioca pearls under cold water and soak them in warm water for at least 30 minutes.
In a large pot, combine the soaked tapioca pearls with 2 cups of water. Bring to a boil then reduce heat to low and simmer until they are soft but not falling apart, about 15-20 minutes.
Drain the tapioca pearls and set aside. In a wok or frying pan, heat sesame oil over medium-high heat. Add garlic cloves and red pepper flakes; sauté for about 30 seconds.
Add drained tapioca pearls to the wok with salt, stir well until evenly coated with flavors from spices. Adjust seasoning if needed.
To make it crispy, you can sprinkle a bit of cornstarch over the top before serving or after adding more oil and tossing the mixture in.
Serve hot as an appetizer for brunch.
